description: Warp Compass ontology — the controlled vocabulary and completeness compass. The LLM may ONLY choose from these node/edge types; it may never invent new ones (anything new goes to a BA review queue). This is NOT a list of questions; it is the internal definition of 'what a complete picture looks like'. See docs/02-technical-approach.md §6.2 and docs/DATA-CONTRACTS.md.

node_types.description: A role/level in the org (e.g., Sales Rep, Inventory Lead, Manager). Personas map to roles.
A unit of work a role performs.
A tool/application used to perform an activity (e.g., CRM, spreadsheet).
A document/record/data object produced or consumed by an activity (e.g., quote, order, report).
A trigger that starts an activity (e.g., 'order received', 'month end').
A point where approval/sign-off is required before work proceeds.
A policy/constraint governing how an activity is done.
A pain point / bottleneck experienced at some level.
A wished-for outcome / what someone wishes were different.
A metric an activity is measured by.

taxonomy_registry.description: Governed, hierarchical category codes used as a many-to-many tag on nodes (NOT node identity — see §6.3). Codes become the final document's section numbering (§11). A genuinely new code goes to a pending-taxonomy review queue, never straight into the graph.
